name: Sam Cooke
abstract: Samuel Cooke (né Cook; January 22, 1931 – December 11, 1964) was an American soul singer and songwriter. Considered one of the most influential soul artists of all time, Cooke is commonly referred to as the "King of Soul" for his distinctive vocals, pioneering contributions to the genre, and significance in popular music.

works_created.t: Jesus Be a Fence Around Me

about_quotes.speaker: Alicia Olatuja
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions Sam Cooke only as an example of a song with cultural significance ('It’s 1963 and Sam Cooke sings “A Change is Gonna’ Come.”'), with no indication of personal interaction or relationship.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alicia Olatuja interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 9, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Fields
about_quotes.evidence: I just opened a tour with him... Don Roby... had bought some dates on Sam Cooke... and he wanted a band to back him

about_quotes.speaker: Albert "June" Gardner
about_quotes.evidence: I left New Orleans and I joined Sam. And I stayed with him until he, tragic in his life, he got killed.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert "June" Gardner
about_quotes.evidence: I left New Orleans and I joined Sam. And I stayed with him until he, tragic in his life, he got killed.

about_quotes.speaker: Alvin "Red" Tyler
about_quotes.evidence: we got in touch, uh, with Sam Cook and he said he would open up a little studio for us to rehearse artists that he would bring in and he would give us all fifty dollars a week while we were staying there.

about_quotes.speaker: Tommy Ridgley
about_quotes.evidence: I saw Sam Cooke and Jackie Wilson at the Sam Club... David Thalibuio band, we played before them. Yeah. And when they brought Sam Cooke home, he literally tore the joint upside down.
